samples.user_query: Kids gymnastics programs near me
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun Gymnastics at 955 Kiowa Ave is your spot — they're open 3–8pm Monday through Friday. Call (928) 566-8862 or check fffhavasu.com for class schedules and age groups.

samples.user_query: Tell me about Bridge City Combat
samples.assistant_text: Bridge City Combat is a martial arts gym run by founder Christian Beyers, located at 2143 McCulloch Blvd N, Unit B. They're open until 9pm, though full weekly hours aren't confirmed yet. You'll need to book in person or reach out directly at (928) 716-3009 to get details on classes and pricing. You can also check them out on Instagram at instagram.com/bridgecitycombat.

route_meta.entity: Bridge City Combat (also: Bridge City Combat & Barry Sullins Jiu-Jitsu)

samples.user_query: Events at Sara Park
samples.assistant_text:
Sara Park has a solid BMX scene. Here's what's running:

**BMX Racing & Practice:**
- Local BMX Races happen weekly on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday evenings at 6pm ($10 admission). Registration opens at 6pm, racing starts at 7pm on Thursdays.
- BMX Practice (Tuesday, 5–6:30pm, $5) — open to all skill levels; striders ride free.
- BMX Training (Wednesday, 5–6:15pm, $5) — coached skills sessions.

**Mountain Biking:**
- Mountain Bike Practice runs Mondays, 4:30–6pm (free). Race practice through May 2026.

All programs are at SARA Park BMX Track, 7260 Sara Park Lane. If you're into BMX or dirt biking, there's something most weeknights.

samples.user_query: BMX race times
samples.assistant_text:
The catalog has BMX race schedules but not individual race times (lap times, heat results, etc.). Here's what we've got:

Weekly races run Thursdays 6–9pm at SARA Park BMX Track (registration 6–7pm, racing starts 7pm, Oct–Jun season). There are also races listed for April 21, 23, 28, and 30, all 6–7pm. Admission's $10.

For actual race times and results, contact Lake Havasu City BMX directly at (928) 208-5388 — they'll have the details on who ran what and how fast.

samples.user_query: Swimming lessons for beginners
samples.assistant_text: Aqua Beginnings offers swim lessons for beginners at a private heated outdoor pool — check aquabeginnings.com to book and confirm their current schedule and pricing.

samples.user_query: Dance classes for a 7-year-old
samples.assistant_text: The catalog shows dance recitals and showcases, but not ongoing classes for kids. You'll want to contact ACPA (which runs the Annual Dance Showcase) or Footlite School of Dance directly — Footlite's at Lake Havasu High School Performing Arts Center, 2675 Palo Verde Blvd S. They'd have the class schedules and age groups for a 7-year-old. A quick search or call to either studio should get you started.

samples.user_query: Underground techno tonight?

samples.user_query: Sonics or Flips for fun for a shy 5-year-old?
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun's a solid pick for a shy 5-year-old — they're open Mon–Fri 3–8pm at 955 Kiowa Ave, and you can call (928) 566-8862 to ask about beginner classes that'd let them ease in at their own pace. I don't have Sonics' class structure in the catalog, so check their site at universalgymnasticslakehavasu.com or call (928) 453-1313 to compare what each offers for that age.

samples.user_query: Best place for toddler tumbling
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun Gymnastics is your best bet for toddler tumbling — they're at 955 Kiowa Ave and open 3–8pm Monday through Friday, so call (928) 566-8862 to ask about their toddler classes and pricing.

samples.user_query: Compare Footlite and Ballet Havasu for preschool dance
samples.assistant_text: I've got Ballet Havasu in the catalog — it's at 2735 Maricopa Ave inside The Dance Center, (928) 412-8208, ballethavasu.org — but I don't have Footlite listed or details on their preschool programs. Call Ballet Havasu directly or check their site to compare what each offers for that age group.

samples.user_query: What's the best sushi in town?
samples.assistant_text: I don't have sushi restaurants in the catalog. Try searching "sushi Lake Havasu" or check https://www.golakehavasu.com/ for current dining options.

samples.user_query: Are home prices going down in Havasu?

samples.user_query: What should I do Saturday?
samples.assistant_text: Go bowling at Havasu Lanes Saturday night for Rock & Bowl — black lights, party vibes, and it's the perfect Saturday energy. Open Bowling runs all day if you want an earlier slot, but the cosmic bowling after 6pm is where it's at on a weekend.

samples.user_query: Pick one thing to do with kids this weekend
samples.assistant_text: Hit Altitude Trampoline Park on Saturday — it's open 9am–9pm and a 90-minute open jump session is $19, so the kids can burn energy without breaking the bank.
